reasoning: On January 31, 2025, Myanmar's military junta extended its state of emergency for another six months until July 31, 2025. Furthermore, in March 2025, junta chief Min Aung Hlaing announced that the national elections will be held in December 2025 or January 2026, and a schedule was released reflecting this timeline. Recent reporting up to April 10, 2025, reaffirms that the junta is planning for a December election. Given that the state of emergency extends beyond the resolution date of July 1, 2025, and the newly announced election dates are at least five months after the resolution deadline, it is virtually certain that voting for a national election will not begin before July 1, 2025. Early voting won't start months before a December election. As a result, the probability is set to the lowest possible bound.
